commitMessage:
[Win32] Enable monitor-specific scaling by default

The monitor-specific UI scaling on Windows is disabled by default and
can be activated via a preference.

This change inverts the default enablement: it enables the feature by
default and allows to disable it via preference. The existing
experimental preference is renamed to reset the configuration for every
consumer. All information about the feature being experimental is
removed.
categoryReason: The change directly impacts the default configuration value of a UI preference. 'Configuration' is a suitable new category for such rules.
dslRule:
<!id: win32-monitor-scaling-default-preference>
<!description: Set default value for monitor-specific UI scaling preference to true.>
<!severity: info>
<!minJavaVersion: 8>
<!tags: configuration, preferences, win32>

org.eclipse.core.runtime.preferences.ConfigurationScope.INSTANCE.getNode(org.eclipse.ui.internal.WorkbenchPlugin.PI_WORKBENCH).getBoolean(org.eclipse.ui.IWorkbenchPreferenceConstants.RESCALING_AT_RUNTIME, false)
=>
org.eclipse.core.runtime.preferences.ConfigurationScope.INSTANCE.getNode(org.eclipse.ui.internal.WorkbenchPlugin.PI_WORKBENCH).getBoolean(org.eclipse.ui.IWorkbenchPreferenceConstants.RESCALING_AT_RUNTIME, true)
;;
summary: This commit changes the default value of a `getBoolean` preference from `false` to `true`. This is a direct expression transformation, replacing one boolean literal with another within a method call, which is perfectly supported by the DSL. Other changes like renaming string constants in declarations, refactoring method calls involving removal/insertion of multiple statements, or modifying `.properties` files are beyond the current DSL capabilities. The proposed rule focuses on the atomic, fully-implementable aspect of the change.

commitMessage:
Apply activity filter for primary wizards in the New Wizard dialog

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.ui/issues/2809
categoryReason: The change specifically deals with filtering wizards within the Eclipse Workbench UI, which is a domain-specific concern not covered by existing general Java categories.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs support for inserting new variable declarations and a sequence of statements, where subsequent statements can reference variables declared in preceding inserted statements. Current replacements are limited to single expressions or single statements directly replacing the matched pattern. It also needs a way to express method references for array creation (e.g., `IWizardDescriptor[]::new`) within replacements, although this is a minor point compared to statement insertion.
summary: The commit introduces a new local variable `filteredWizards` by applying an activity filter (`WorkbenchActivityHelper.filterCollection`) to an existing array, then uses this new variable in a subsequent constructor call. This involves inserting a new statement (variable declaration) before an existing statement and modifying the existing statement to use the new variable. The TriggerPattern DSL does not currently support inserting new statements or sequences of statements, or creating and referencing temporary variables within a transformation. This requires a significant extension to the DSL's capabilities beyond simple expression/statement replacement.

irrelevantReason: The commit fixes spelling errors in Javadoc comments. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for structural code transformations (expressions, statements, annotations) and does not support arbitrary text replacement within comments or non-code string literals.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to be extended with capabilities for text-based pattern matching and replacement within comments or string literals, potentially using regex or a dedicated 'spellCheck' guard, which is beyond its current scope.
summary: This commit is solely for correcting spelling errors in Javadoc comments. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for Java code transformations, not for text editing within comments, thus it's not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Ensure listeners are properly removed from the ScopedPreferenceStore

Currently the ScopedPreferenceStore adds two kind of listeners but only
removes one. Even worse it determines the object from removing listeners
by lookup the target again, so in some cases it is assumed that the
listener was already removed.

This now adds a composite listener that handles both cases and remember
the objects where listener where added. These listeners are then removed
on disposal.
categoryReason: The change is a refactoring of listener management within an Eclipse UI/preferences component (`ScopedPreferenceStore`), which falls under the existing 'Eclipse-Workbench' category.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support: 1. Introducing new classes (e.g., private static nested helper classes). 2. Moving code from anonymous inner classes or lambda expressions into methods of a newly created class. 3. Replacing multiple, disparate statements (like separate listener additions and removals) with a single, object-oriented construct (e.g., instantiating a helper class). This level of structural refactoring, involving new type creation and significant code reorganization, is beyond the current DSL's capabilities for expression/statement replacement.
summary: The commit refactors how listeners are managed in `ScopedPreferenceStore` by consolidating two distinct listener types (node change and preference change listeners) into a new private static nested helper class (`EclipsePreferencesListener`). This involves creating a new type, moving existing anonymous listener logic into methods of this new class, and modifying the instantiation and disposal pattern. This is a complex structural refactoring that introduces new types and reorganizes multiple statements, which is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Fix "Preferences" Context Menu Entry Label

Actions that open a dialog should be suffixed with "...". In the
"Progress" view this was missing.
irrelevantReason: The change is in a `.properties` file, which contains string literals used for localization. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for Java code transformations (Abstract Syntax Tree manipulation), not string literal changes in property files.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need to support matching and rewriting patterns in `.properties` files, which is outside its current scope for Java code transformation.
summary: This commit modifies a string in a `.properties` file. The TriggerPattern DSL is for Java code transformations, making this change irrelevant for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Replace usages of new Image(device, width, height)

Replacing it with imageGcDrawer
categoryReason: The transformation involves SWT API, which is a core UI toolkit in Eclipse. Existing plugins like `sandbox_jface_cleanup` (which often interacts with SWT) fall under this broader category.
languageChangeNeeded:
The current DSL supports single-expression or single-statement replacements. This transformation requires:
1. Matching a sequence of multiple statements (Image constructor, GC constructor, GC operations, GC dispose).
2. Replacing this sequence with a new sequence of statements (ImageGcDrawer lambda declaration, Image constructor with lambda).
3. Generating a multi-line lambda expression as part of the replacement. The DSL explicitly forbids multi-line blocks in replacements. This capability would require significant extensions to the DSL's matching and rewriting semantics, moving from expression-level to block-level transformations.
summary: This commit replaces a multi-statement pattern (creating an `Image`, creating a `GC`, drawing, disposing `GC`) with a single `Image` constructor that takes an `ImageGcDrawer` lambda. This kind of transformation, involving multiple statements and generating a multi-line lambda body, is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which is limited to single-expression/statement replacements. It would require significant DSL extensions for block-level transformations.

commitMessage:
Avoid implementations of ImageDescriptor.getImageData()

ImageDescriptor.getImageData() is deprecated and sub-classes are advised
to stop re-implementing it and instead implement getImageData(int).
The still existing implementations just replicate the default
implementation and therefore can just be removed.

Additionally implementation of
ImageDescriptor.getMissingImageDescriptor() is simplified and
ImageDescriptor implementations are cleaned-up.
categoryReason: The change involves adopting a newer Java language feature (Pattern Matching for instanceof) to simplify code.
dslRule:
<!id: modernize-java16-pmfi-negated>
<!description: Convert negated instanceof checks to Java 16+ Pattern Matching for instanceof>
<!severity: info>
<!minJavaVersion: 16>
<!tags: modernization, java16, pattern-matching>

!( $target instanceof $Type ) :: sourceVersionGE(16)
=>
!( $target instanceof $Type $varName )
;;
summary: The commit primarily introduces Java 16 Pattern Matching for `instanceof` (PMFI) in a negated `if` condition, transforming `!(o instanceof Type)` to `!(o instanceof Type other)`. This specific part of the change is directly expressible as a DSL rule. The DSL can match the `instanceof` expression and replace it with its PMFI equivalent. The `$varName` placeholder in the replacement is expected to be handled by the JDT transformation engine, which will infer a suitable name (e.g., 'other', 'descriptor') for the new pattern variable. Other changes in the commit, such as altering field modifiers (`public` to `private`) or removing entire method declarations, are not supported by the current DSL's expression/single-statement replacement capabilities. The proposed DSL rule will only address the `instanceof` expression transformation, not the subsequent removal of explicit cast statements, which would require multi-statement transformations (currently unsupported).

commitMessage:
Replace deprecated Platform.asLocalURL with FileLocator.toFileURL

Replaces the old URL handling system with FileLocator.toFileURL to eliminate dependency on deprecated API.
categoryReason: The change involves replacing a deprecated API from `org.eclipse.core.runtime.Platform` with a modern equivalent from `org.eclipse.core.runtime.FileLocator`, which are core Eclipse APIs relevant to the Workbench context.
dslRule:
/*!id: replace-platform-aslocalurl>
<!description: Replace deprecated Platform.asLocalURL with FileLocator.toFileURL.>
<!severity: warning>
<!minJavaVersion: 8>
<!tags: eclipse-api, deprecation, modernization, platform-plugin>

org.eclipse.core.runtime.Platform.asLocalURL($url)
=>
org.eclipse.core.runtime.FileLocator.toFileURL($url)
;;
summary: This commit replaces a deprecated `Platform.asLocalURL` call with `FileLocator.toFileURL`. This is a straightforward API replacement suitable for a GREEN DSL rule. The replacement API has existed since before Java 8, so no `sourceVersionGE` guard is needed in the rule body. The rule is highly reusable for Eclipse plugin modernization efforts.
